name: Marinated Fried Green Tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare and start marinating the tomatoes early in the day.
2. Cut the stem end out of each tomato and cut it in half lengthwise.
3. Trim off a small slice from the rounded side of each tomato half and discard it.
4. Cut each half, lengthwise, into 2 slices, each about 1/2 inch thick.
5. Pack the tomato slices into a bowl.
6. In a saucepan, slowly stir the 2 cups of water into the mustard powder and tomato paste until the mixture is smooth.
7. Add the 2 tablespoons of oil, vinegar, soy sauce, honey, 2 teaspoons of salt, celery salt, and cayenne to the saucepan.
8. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, then simmer it uncovered for 5 minutes.
9. Pour the hot marinade over the tomato slices in the bowl and place a saucer with a weight on top to keep them submerged. Allow them to marinate at least 6 hours.
10. Just before serving, drain the tomatoes in a large strainer set over a bowl.
11. Measure out the drained marinade liquid and add enough water if necessary to bring the total measurement to 3 cups.
12. Dissolve the cornstarch in the liquid and bring it to a simmer in a heavy saucepan over medium heat while stirring constantly with a rubber spatula.
13. Reduce the heat slightly and simmer for an additional 2 minutes, still stirring constantly.
14. Heat 1/4 inch of oil in a heavy, 10-inch frying pan over medium-high heat.
15. Work in batches of 8 slices; lightly salt both sides of each slice, dredge it in flour, and place it on a plate.
16. Fry the floured tomato slices for 2 to 2 1/2 minutes on each side until they are golden brown.
17. Do not overcook them, as they retain heat and continue cooking after removal from the pan.
18. Drain the fried slices briefly on paper towels.
19. Transfer them to a warm dinner plate and serve immediately with a small bowl of the marinade sauce.
